---
title: "Canada Data Observatory"
subtitle: "Interactive metrics for informed civic conversation"
---

Canada Data Observatory provides interactive, regularly updated charts and
downloadable data on the state of the country — its population, economy, cost of
living, incomes, public finances, health, education, environment, and well-being. All data comes
from official sources — primarily [Statistics Canada](https://www.statcan.gc.ca/)
and the [OECD](https://data.oecd.org/) — and is updated automatically each week.

Where possible, Canada is shown alongside a broad set of 17 OECD peer countries
(not just the G7) to provide meaningful international context.

This is an open, non-partisan project. It presents data without policy advocacy.
Data files are freely downloadable for anyone to use — students, journalists,
researchers, and anyone who wants accessible Canadian data.

---

## Start with these questions

New here? These are some of the big questions this data can help you explore —
each links to the section behind it:

- [**Is Canada becoming less affordable?**](housing/) — inflation, house prices, rents, and household debt
- [**Are incomes keeping up?**](income/) — wages, real incomes, and poverty
- [**How do Canadian living standards compare with peer countries?**](economics/) — GDP per capita, productivity, and jobs
- [**Where is population growth coming from?**](population/) — immigration, temporary residents, and natural increase
- [**How big is government, and what does it spend on?**](fiscal/) — debt, deficits, the public-service workforce, and federal spending
- **How is Canada doing on [health](health/), the [environment](environment/), and [well-being](wellbeing/)?** — life expectancy, emissions, and life satisfaction

---

## Topics

::::{.columns}

:::{.column width="32%"}
### [Population](population/)
How many people live in Canada, how the population is changing, and why.

*Source: Statistics Canada*
:::

:::{.column width="2%"}
:::

:::{.column width="32%"}
### [Economy & Jobs](economics/)
GDP per capita, labour productivity, and unemployment, compared with peers.

*Sources: OECD, Statistics Canada*
:::

:::{.column width="2%"}
:::

:::{.column width="32%"}
### [Housing & Cost of Living](housing/)
Inflation, house prices, affordability, and household debt.

*Sources: OECD, Statistics Canada*
:::

::::

::::{.columns}

:::{.column width="32%"}
### [Income & Inequality](income/)
Real incomes and wages, the Gini coefficient, and poverty rates.

*Sources: OECD, Statistics Canada*
:::

:::{.column width="2%"}
:::

:::{.column width="32%"}
### [Health](health/)
Life expectancy and how much countries spend on health care.

*Source: OECD*
:::

:::{.column width="2%"}
:::

:::{.column width="32%"}
### [Education & Innovation](science/)
R&D spending, researchers, and educational attainment.

*Source: OECD*
:::

::::

::::{.columns}

:::{.column width="32%"}
### [Environment](environment/)
CO₂ emissions, the energy mix, and renewable energy.

*Sources: OECD, Our World in Data*
:::

:::{.column width="2%"}
:::

:::{.column width="32%"}
### [Society & Well-being](wellbeing/)
Life satisfaction and the factors that underlie it.

*Source: World Happiness Report*
:::

:::{.column width="2%"}
:::

:::{.column width="32%"}
### [Government & Public Finances](fiscal/)
Debt and deficits, the size of the public-service workforce, and what the federal government spends.

*Sources: Statistics Canada, OECD, Treasury Board*
:::

::::

---

## Principles

- **Official sources only** — Statistics Canada, OECD, and other authoritative agencies
- **Broad peer comparisons** — 17 OECD countries, not just the G7
- **Automatically updated** — data pipelines run weekly
- **Open and downloadable** — all cleaned data available as CSV
- **No policy advocacy** — we present the data, sources, and methods to support informed discussion, not to push conclusions
